rows.Name: Tree
rows.Behavior: Neutral, drops fruit or Crate on death as well as 3 Materials.

rows.Name: Baby Alien
rows.Behavior: Chases the character, deals damage on touch

rows.Name: Chaser
rows.Behavior: Chases the character, deals damage on touch, spawns in groups

rows.Name: Spitter
rows.Behavior: Runs away from the character if too close, fires projectiles that deal damage on hit

rows.Name: Charger
rows.Behavior: Chases the character, can charge with cooldown of 2.5-3.5 seconds, damages on touch

rows.Name: Pursuer
rows.Behavior: Chases the character, damages on touch, keeps getting faster each second

rows.Name: Bruiser
rows.Behavior: Chases the character, can charge with cooldown of 2.5-3.5 seconds, damages on touch

rows.Name: Buffer
rows.Behavior: Runs away from the character, Buffs other enemies' health by 150%, damage by 25%, and speed by 50%. Buffed enemies are outlined in red

rows.Name: Fly
rows.Behavior: Moves around player. Fires projectiles in random directions when hit by the player's projectiles. Melee Weapons can safely hit the Fly without spawning Projectiles.

rows.Name: Healer
rows.Behavior: Moves around enemies, healing enemies around it for 100(increasing by 10 each wave)

rows.Name: Looter
rows.Behavior: Runs away from the character, drops a Loot Crate and 8 Materials on death Has 10% chance to spawn 25 seconds into a wave, and every 15 seconds thereafter.

rows.Name: Helmet Alien
rows.Behavior: Chases the character, deals damage on touch

rows.Name: Fin Alien
rows.Behavior: Chases the character, deals damage on touch

rows.Name: Spawner
rows.Behavior: Spawns 3 Junkie Aliens on death

rows.Name: Junkie
rows.Behavior: Moves around player, fires projectiles near player that deals damage

rows.Name: Horned Bruiser
rows.Behavior: Chases the character, can charge with cooldown of 2.5-3.5 seconds, damages on touch

rows.Name: Horned Charger
rows.Behavior: Chases the character, can charge with cooldown of 2.5-3.5 seconds, damages on touch

rows.Name: Slasher Egg
rows.Behavior: Stays still, dies and spawns a Slasher after 5 seconds unless killed

rows.Name: Slasher
rows.Behavior: Follows the character, runs away if too close, slashes vertically dealing damage from medium range

rows.Name: Tentacle
rows.Behavior: Follows the character, runs away if too close, attacks in "V" shape from medium range

rows.Name: Lamprey
rows.Behavior: Spawns on the wave Bait is bought. Follows the player, charges to the player every 1.5 seconds, shooting projectiles to its left and right during the charge

rows.Name: Gobbler
rows.Behavior: Target materials to eat them, dealing damage on touch. Eating makes it grow. Has 3% chance to spawn 10 seconds into a wave. Mutation 0: Fast but fragile. Killing it makes it drop all the materials it ate. Mutation 1: Loses speed but gains max HP. Killing it makes it drop twice the amount of materials it ate. Mutation 2: Loses more speed but gains even more max HP. Killing it makes it drop three times the amount of materials it ate.

rows.Name: Rhino
rows.Behavior: Mutation 0: Charges to the player every 2 seconds, shooting projectiles to its left and right during the charge Mutation 1(60%HP/25 sec): Charges to the player every 1.3 seconds for shorter distance, shooting projectiles to its left and right during the charge, at the start of its charge fires extra 2 projectiles

rows.Name: Butcher
rows.Behavior: Mutation 0: Slashes 4 times every 1.25 seconds around the player pointing to the player. Mutation 1(70%HP/25 sec): Slashes 8 times every second in a random pattern around the player in a large area. Mutation 2(40%HP/40 sec): Slashes 3 times every 0.75 seconds around the player randomly in a large area that deals 33% less damage.

rows.Name: Monk
rows.Behavior: Mutation 0: Spawns 15 Slasher Eggs Mutation 1(10 sec): Shoots 5 projectiles each second to the player Mutation 2(30 sec): Last 10 seconds, runs away from the player, spawning Tentacles Returns to Mutation 1

rows.Name: Croc
rows.Behavior: Mutation 0: Charges to the player every second creating 2 slashes around itself Mutation 1(60%HP/25 sec): Charges to the player every second creating a circle of projectiles around the player (making it harder to dodge the charge)

rows.Name: Colossus
rows.Behavior: Mutation 0: Chases the player - spawns 50 moving projectiles in a big random area every second Mutation 1(60%HP/25 sec): Random walk pattern - spawns circle of projectiles around player that moves every 0.5 seconds

rows.Name: Mantis
rows.Behavior: Mutation 0: Chases the player - Slashes 6 times every 1.25 seconds around itself Mutation 1(60%HP/25 sec): Chases the player - Charges to player every 1.3 seconds creating slashes around it

rows.Name: Mother
rows.Behavior: Mutation 0: Chases the player - Creates a 4-pronged slash moving away from themself every 1.4 seconds Mutation 1(60%HP/25 sec): Moves away from the player - Double-slashes every 0.65 seconds in a random pattern around the player; Spawns ~9 Fin Aliens per second (a group of 6 every 0.65 seconds)

rows.Name: Gargoyle
rows.Behavior: Mutation 0: Random walk pattern - spawns a big circle of projectiles around itself every 0.3 seconds Mutation 1(60%HP/30 sec): Chases the player - spawning 12 projectiles around itself in random pattern every 0.4 seconds Mutation 2(30%HP/45 sec): Chases the player - spawns a 3 of projectiles in random directions every 0.01 - 0.15 seconds

rows.Name: Predator
rows.Behavior: Mutation 0: Chases the player and dashes. It also surrounds itself in several projectiles that circle the boss.  Mutation 1(50%HP/45 sec): No longer dashes but starts to emit projectiles in every direction every so often.

rows.Name: Invoker
rows.Behavior: Mutation 0: Every two seconds creates a area of projectiles around the players that spawn after a second of priming. Mutation 1(75%HP/30 sec): More projectiles spawn in a much wider area around the player. Mutation 2(40%HP/60 sec): Speed increases by 150%, runs all around the area and creates a circle of projectiles around the player.

rows.Name: Anemone
rows.Behavior: Wanders slowly and creates a circle of projectiles around that move towards itself.

rows.Name: Anglerfish
rows.Behavior: Wanders slowly around until a player comes close, then will chase it charging up to 3 times before briefly stopping and charge again.

rows.Name: Blobfish
rows.Behavior: Slowly wanders around until killed, then spawns 4 Lampreys and a Sea Pig. If the last player weapon hit was done by a melee weapon, it spawns only 2 Lampreys.

rows.Name: Clam
rows.Behavior: Runs away if the player is too close, Fires a slow projectile that may directly target the player or somewhere near.

rows.Name: Colossal Squid
rows.Behavior: Closes in on the player and fires two crossing spikes toward them.

rows.Name: Crab
rows.Behavior: Closes in on the player and then fires a spike and moves backwards.

rows.Name: Diplocaulus
rows.Behavior: Slowly wanders the map leaving behind slasher eggs every few seconds.

rows.Name: Dragonfish
rows.Behavior: Shoots lines of bullets in the players direction

rows.Name: Goblin Shark
rows.Behavior: Charges to where the player is moving

rows.Name: Hermit
rows.Behavior: Wanders around and heals enemies

rows.Name: Iron Lung
rows.Behavior: Iron Lung is spawned by Stargazers, who will seek it out and enter it. After a few seconds of mutation (during which it bobs and turns red), a Dragonfish spawns.

rows.Name: Lobster
rows.Behavior: Chases the player and takes reduced damage from all sources.

rows.Name: Looting Pig
rows.Behavior: Runs around, drops a crate on death

rows.Name: Narwhal
rows.Behavior: Closes in on the player and can charge toward them, firing a spike in front of it.

rows.Name: Plankton
rows.Behavior: Chases the player and sometimes charges a small distance .

rows.Name: Pufferfish
rows.Behavior: Explodes into 10 projectiles when killed by non melee damage or when it dies after being charmed.

rows.Name: Sea Pig
rows.Behavior: Wanders around, drops a fair number of materials and gives 1 curse on death

rows.Name: Shrimp
rows.Behavior: Chases the character, deals damage on touch

rows.Name: Stargazer
rows.Behavior: Moves toward its Iron Lung, becomes buffed if its iron lung dies

rows.Name: Stonefish
rows.Behavior: Slowly wanders the map buffing enemies and spawning six projectiles around the player every few seconds. Buffed enemies gain +150% health, +25% damage, and +50% speed, and are outlined in red.

rows.Name: Vampire Squid
rows.Behavior: Chases the player while firing two projectiles in front of itself.

rows.Name: Viperfish
rows.Behavior: Starts growing after spawning, after a few seconds starts chasing the player.

rows.Name: Walrus
rows.Behavior: Charges towards the player every few seconds.

rows.Name: Bat
rows.Behavior: Mutation 0: Closes in on the player and spawns a circle of projectiles around them. These projectiles move toward the player at varying speeds before disappearing.  Mutation 1(60%HP/25 sec): Closes in on the player and fires a twisting circle of projectiles from itself toward the player.

rows.Name: Giant
rows.Behavior: Mutation 0: Chases the player, with 2 lines of projectiles slowly extending and orbiting around it.  Mutation 1(70%HP/25 sec): Chases the player, still with 2 lines of projectiles orbiting around it, and fires 2 parallel spikes toward the player.  Mutation 2(40%HP/55 sec): Chases the player, charging toward them regularly.

rows.Name: Giant Isopod
rows.Behavior: Mutation 0: Chases the player and fires numerous projectiles forming a spiral around itself.  Mutation 1(60%HP/25 sec): Chases the player and fires a burst of projectiles toward them.

rows.Name: Impaled Worm
rows.Behavior: Mutation 0: Moves randomly and charges to a random position near the player, firing 3 consecutive spikes toward them.  Mutation 1(60%HP/25 sec): Chases the player and regularly charges toward them while firing numerous spikes in all directions from itself.

rows.Name: Jellyfish
rows.Behavior: Mutation 0: Chases the player while having 4 projectiles orbiting around itself and regularly spawning a large circle of projectiles moving inward.  Mutation 1(60%HP/25 sec): Remains stationary while still having 4 orbiting projectiles and spawning a larger circle of projectiles around itself. Also fires lines of projectiles toward the player.

rows.Name: Megalodon
rows.Behavior: Mutation 0: Chases the player and regularly charges toward them while firing bursts of projectiles.  Mutation 1(60%HP/25 sec): Chases the player and charges toward them more frequently while firing circles of projectiles from itself.

rows.Name: Prisoner
rows.Behavior: Mutation 0: Chases the player and spawns a small circle of 6 projectiles that move inward indefinitely.  Mutation 1(75%HP/20 sec): Closes in on the player, spawning 2 consecutive circles of projectiles around them.  Mutation 2(40%HP/40 sec): Chases the player while continuing to spawn 2 consecutive circles of projectiles around them.

rows.Name: Spider Crab
rows.Behavior: Mutation 0: Moves randomly and spawns numerous crabs.  Mutation 1(85%HP/12 sec): Closes in on the player and fires spikes toward them.  Mutation 2(50%HP/35 sec): Closes in on the player and fires 4 spikes toward them in 4 directions.

rows.Name: Turtle
rows.Behavior: Mutation 0: Chases the player while firing 8 spikes around them, heading inward.  Mutation 1(60%HP/25 sec): Closes in on the player, regularly firing 3 spikes toward them in a random pattern.

rows.Name: Dead Whale
rows.Behavior: Mutation 0: Moves randomly and fires 3 waves of 6 projectiles toward the player at regular intervals.  Mutation 1(70%HP/20 sec): Chases the player, regularly charging toward them while firing numerous projectiles at varying speeds in all directions.  Mutation 2(40%HP/45 sec): Chases the player more frequently, charging more often while spawning a large circle of projectiles around itself.

rows.Name: Eel
rows.Behavior: Mutation 0: Chases the player, firing a stream of projectiles.  Mutation 1(70%HP/25 sec): Moves randomly and spawns a spiral of projectiles centered on the player, which then move toward them.  Mutation 2(40%HP/55 sec): Chases the player while continuing to spawn spirals of projectiles and having four lines of projectiles orbiting around it.

rows.Name: Corrupted Tree
rows.Behavior: No longer spawns in the Game. But exists in Gamefiles. Stands in place, creates circling projectiles that deals damage

Shop.section: Rarity of Shop Items and Luck
Rarity of Shop Items and Luck > Rarities with 0 Luck
Rarity of Shop Items and Luck > Rarities with 50 Luck
Rarity of Shop Items and Luck > Rarities with 100 Luck

rows.Stat: Only gets offered Tier 2 or above Weapons
Shops always sell at least one weapon

rows.Notable Items: Arms Dealer - -95% Weapon Prices, Weapons are destroyed when entering the shop.  Builder - Won't get offered Structures.  Bull - Won't get offered Weapons.  Demon - Pays for items Using Max HP.  Diver - Harpoon Gun Costs and Recycles for 1 material.  Fisherman - Shop always sells a Bait, -100% Bait prices.  Gangster - Can't Lock items, can steal from the shop.  Vagabond - Can't get offered weapons that it already has of a lower tier, and if you have a tier 4, you can't get offered that weapon at all.

rows.Stats: Max HP
HP Regeneration
Life Steal
Damage
DLC Stats
Curse

rows.Name: Max HP
rows.Effects: Amount of damage you can take before dying
rows.Negative stat effect: Acts like the stat is at 1

rows.Name: HP Regeneration
rows.Effects: Passive healing that restores 1 HP per n seconds, where the healing interval n becomes shorter with increasing HP Regeneration.  The first point of HP regeneration gives 0.20 HP/s and all further points increase it by 0.089 HP/s(details)
rows.Negative stat effect: Acts like the stat is at 0

rows.Name: Life Steal
rows.Effects: Your attacks have a x% chance to heal you for 1HP.  The character's Life Steal chance is added to each weapon individually.
rows.Limit: Can only trigger once per 0.1 second for a maximum of 10HP/second
rows.Negative stat effect: Decreases the life steal chance of all weapons to minimum of 0

rows.Name: Damage
rows.Effects: Increases all damage dealt by 1% per point in Damage.  Does not affect Engineering structures.
rows.Negative stat effect: Decreases damage by 1% per negative point instead. The minimum damage caused by a weapon or effect is always 1

rows.Name: Melee Damage
rows.Effects: Modifies the base attack damage of weapons, if their damage scales with Melee Damage.  Some items deal damage based on this stat.
rows.Negative stat effect: Decreases the base attack damage. The minimum damage caused by a weapon or effect is always 1

rows.Name: Ranged Damage
rows.Effects: Modifies the base attack damage of weapons, if their damage scales with Ranged Damage.  Some items deal damage based on this stat.
rows.Negative stat effect: Decreases the base attack damage. The minimum damage caused by a weapon or effect is always 1

rows.Name: Elemental Damage
rows.Effects: Modifies the base attack damage of weapons, if their damage scales with Elemental Damage.  Some items deal damage based on this stat.
rows.Negative stat effect: Decreases the base attack damage. The minimum damage caused by a weapon or effect is always 1

rows.Name: Attack Speed
rows.Effects: You attack x% faster. Also applies to Ranged Weapons but not Structures
rows.Limit: Diminishing Returns at higher values, can't attack faster than 12 times per second, slower for melee weapons.
rows.Negative stat effect: You attack slower. Negative attack speed is calculated differently and doesn't impact you as much as positive attack speed.

rows.Name: Crit Chance
rows.Effects: Your weapons have an x increased chance to deal a critical hit.
rows.Limit: Maximum 100% chance to do critical hits on a hit.
rows.Negative stat effect: The amount is subtracted from the base crit chance of your weapon. Minimum is 0% chance in total to crit.

rows.Name: Engineering
rows.Effects: Increases the power of your structures. The other stats don't affect structures but specials do (piercing, bouncing, explosion etc.)
rows.Negative stat effect: Your structures have x decreased power down to a minimum of 1 damage or healing

rows.Name: Range
rows.Effects: The max range of your weapons is increased by x. A higher range also increases the cooldown of melee weapons.(longer travel distance)
rows.Negative stat effect: Your weapons have x less range. (Halved for melee weapons). Minimum of 25 range.

rows.Name: Armor
rows.Effects: You reduce incoming damage by x%. Each point of armor means it takes 6.66% more damage to kill you.
rows.Negative stat effect: The damage you take is increased by x% instead of decreased.

rows.Name: Dodge
rows.Effects: You have a x% chance to dodge attacks.

rows.Name: Speed
rows.Effects: You move x% faster.
rows.Negative stat effect: You move x% slower, coming to a standstill at -100% or more.

rows.Name: Luck
rows.Effects: You have an x% more chance of finding items or consumables when you kill enemies. Also increases the rarity of the items in the shop and level upgrades.
rows.Negative stat effect: You have an x% less chance of finding items or consumables when you kill enemies. Also decreases the rarity of the items in the shop and level upgrades.

rows.Name: Harvesting
rows.Effects: You earn x materials and XP at the end of a wave. Increased by 5% every time it activates. (Rounded up)
rows.Negative stat effect: You lose x materials and XP at the end of a wave. The 5% Increment doesn't happen.

rows.Name: Curse
rows.Effects: Gives enemies a chance to spawn cursed with a purple outline. Cursed enemies have +25% Damage, +15% Speed, and +150% HP +2% per Curse Stat additional HP. They also drop 33% additional Materials. Causes items and weapons in the Shop to have a chance to spawn enhanced with a curse buff, increasing their stats significantly and decreasing their downsides.
rows.Limit: Diminishing Returns on chance to spawn enemies and items. Can't go above 50% Chance to spawn cursed enemies, 15% to spawn cursed items. Cursed HP scaling maxes out at 300 Curse.

rows.Description: Increases or decreases the healing done by consumables.
rows.Notable Items: Alien Worm, Jerky, Lemonade, Weird Food

rows.Description: Chance of being healed by 1 when picking up Materials. Currently only used by Cute Monkey.
Increases or decreases all forms of Experience you earn.
rows.Notable Items: Bean Teacher, Black Belt, Celery Tea, Diploma, Feather, Peacock, Scar

rows.Description: Increases or decreases the range at which you pick up materials and consumables.
rows.Notable Items: Alien Tongue, Cauldron, Gnome, Little Frog,

rows.Description: Increases or decreases Shop Prices.
Increases or decreases the damage of Explosions
Increases or decreases the size of Explosions
Gives projectiles additional Bounces
Gives projectiles additional Piercing
Increases or decreases the piercing damage falloff for projectile weapons.
Multiplies the damage you do against Bosses and Elites.
Increases or decreases the rate of turrets attacking, Landmines spawning, and Gardens spawning Consumables.
Increases or decreases the range of Structures.
Increases or decreases the rate of which Burn ticks for damage.
Increases the amount of times Burn spreads.
Increases or decreases the distance enemies are knockbacked back when hit.
rows.Notable Items: Ball and Chain, Banner, Coil, Black Belt, Boxing Glove

rows.Description: Increases the chance of gaining double materials when picking up Materials
Provides an amount of free reroll at the beginning of each Shop
Increases the amount of Trees that spawn. See the Wiki page for details.
Increases or decreases the amount of Enemies that spawn during waves.
rows.Notable Items: Black Flag, Candle, Gentle Alien, Mouse, White Flag

rows.Description: Increases or decreases the speed of Enemies that spawn during waves.
Increases or decreases the price of rerolling in the [[Shop}]] and when choosing Upgrades

Endless_Mode.section: The Endless Factor > The Endless Factor At different waves

rows.Name: Dying
rows.Notes & Tips: See the Wiki Page for a list of items that give negative HP Regeneration.

rows.Name: Agriculture
rows.Notes & Tips: Can happen on all characters, but Pacifist or Entrepreneur are good candidates. If you see an early Crown or Tractor, that run has a good shot of reaching 200+ Harvesting.

rows.Name: Hoarder
rows.Notes & Tips: Easiest with Saver, who likes to hold on to Materials.

rows.Name: Builder
rows.Challenge: Get 5 turrets on the map simultaneously

rows.Name: Perfect Vision
rows.Notes & Tips: Ranger has an advantage here. They have +50 Range and are tagged for Range Items.

rows.Name: Fireworks
rows.Challenge: Kill 15 enemies with a single explosion
rows.Notes & Tips: Easiest with Bull, who regularly emits a single explosion

rows.Name: Recycling
rows.Notes & Tips: This one rarely happens naturally. You can either make a dummy run where you just buy 12 weapons, sell them, and then lose the run. Or you can buy cheap tier 1 weapons over the duration of a regular run. Entrepreneur can be quite good at this, since you have to spend all your money, and you get more money back from recycling.

rows.Name: Slow
rows.Notes & Tips: Playing Old gives you the first -10%. Then you can use the Blunt Weapon set to get more. Otherwise just buy items with negative Speed. The Page got a list of items with negative speed.

rows.Name: Hungry
rows.Challenge: Pick up 20 consumables during a run

rows.Name: Advanced Technology
rows.Challenge: Reach 10 Ranged Damage and get 3 structures  at the same time

rows.Name: Giant Slayer
rows.Challenge: Kill a boss or an elite in less than 15 seconds
rows.Notes & Tips: This one can be a bit tricky. Look for characters that can deal amazing damage with ranged builds. Loud or Soldier can deal a ton of damage to elites. Try a ranged build that deals a lot of single target damage like for example a build using SMG or Revolver.

rows.Name: Forest
rows.Challenge: Finish a wave with 10 or more living trees
rows.Notes & Tips: This one doesn't happen naturally. The only easy way to get it is by playing Explorer for some waves until the wave duration is long enough. Then play through a wave destroying as few trees as possible.

rows.Name: Bourgeoisie
rows.Challenge: Get 3 tier IV weapons at once

rows.Name: Scavenger
rows.Challenge: Get 10 different tier I items during a run
rows.Notes & Tips: The 10 different items must be Items and not Weapons. Sometimes "items" refers to both, but not here.

rows.Name: Fast Learner
rows.Notes & Tips: Play a few waves as Mutant

rows.Name: Experimentation
rows.Notes & Tips: You can play a game with Gladiator, or it can be done in the first shop with Arms Dealer

rows.Name: Magic and Machinery
rows.Challenge: Reach 10 Elemental Damage and get 3 structures at the same time
rows.Notes & Tips: Can be done with any elemental build by just buying some Structures. An engineering build on Apprentice gets it automatically as you level up.

rows.Name: Blood Drinker
rows.Notes & Tips: Easiest way is to play as Sick and buy all Lifesteal you see in the shop.

rows.Name: Arms Dealer
rows.Challenge: Win a run with Arms Dealer

rows.Name: One Armed
rows.Challenge: Win a run with One Armed

rows.Name: Well Rounded
rows.Challenge: Win a run with Well Rounded

rows.Name: Give the money back
rows.Notes: The Gobbler appears randomly (3% chance after 10 seconds into the wave)

rows.Name: Ew, what's that smell?!
rows.Challenge: Win a run in Danger 3 or more
rows.Notes: System, allows you to banish 8 items from the shop per run

rows.Name: Let's try this wave again
rows.Challenge: During the last 5 seconds of a wave after wave 2, press 'Return to Main Menu'. Then press 'Resume' to try the wave again.

rows.Name: Trick or Treat?
rows.Challenge: Win a run without locking any items in the shop.
rows.Notes: Playing as a character like Gangster who can't lock items will make this simple. Lower difficulty levels also work.

rows.Name: Mmm, so juicy!
rows.Challenge: Collect 100 fruits while at maximum health
rows.Notes: Characters who benefit from fruit at max health like Glutton or Farmer may get this achievement passively. Also consider Penguin.

rows.Name: Smells like something's burning?
rows.Challenge: Kill 30 enemies by burning them in a single wave
rows.Notes: A fire-focused Mage build should pick this up easily. Alternatively, as this seems to count enemies who died while on fire, any character with a Scared Sausage and lots of damage should do it.

rows.Name: BooOOooOoo~
rows.Notes: Ghost is recommended

rows.Name: Crit Happens
rows.Notes: This is easiest to achieve in Endless mode by going for Crit Chance upgrades after you have a build going, as 100% Crit Chance is much higher than you'd normally aim for in a game.

rows.Name: Smelly Feet
rows.Notes and Tips: Unlocks automatically by playing multiple runs.

rows.Name: Herbalist
rows.Challenge: Collect 250 consumables by the end of wave 20
rows.Notes and Tips: Playing a character based around consumables, like Farmer or Glutton makes this much easier to achieve.

rows.Name: Unstoppable Force
rows.Challenge: Hit at least 25 enemies with a single melee attack
rows.Notes and Tips: Use big slow weapons such as Spear or Anchor and hit a ton of enemies. Look for a Horde Wave. You can also use Loud. The additional enemies makes it much easier to hit 25.

rows.Name: Barbecue
rows.Challenge: Have at least 25 enemies burning at the same time
rows.Notes and Tips: Use a Fireball or Flamethrower to spread a ton of fire. You can try to find a Horde wave. Pacifist is great for this, since the burning enemies don't die.

rows.Name: Unlucky
rows.Challenge: Reroll 10 times in a single shop
rows.Notes and Tips: This one can be done at the first shop. Just reroll 10 times. Pick a Character with starting harvesting like Saver for an easier time.

rows.Name: Overkill
rows.Challenge: Deal 1000 damage to an enemy in a single hit
rows.Notes and Tips: Sailor with an Anchor is quite great here. The +200% damage to cursed enemies makes it easy to deal 1000 damage.

rows.Name: Uncorrupted
rows.Notes and Tips: Use short ranged Melee weapons and avoid killing the Sea Pig / Curse Hoarders. They give +1 Curse when killed. Avoid buying items that give curse. You can play Pacifist for an easier time. The Crash Zone map also have fewer enemies that give Curse on death.

rows.Name: Cautious
rows.Challenge: Kill 1000 enemies that are more than 700 range away from you
rows.Notes and Tips: You get it over multiple games. Use weapons like Slingshot or Play Lucky/ Hunter to hit enemies all over the map.

rows.Name: Blind Greed
rows.Notes and Tips: Start a game as Soldier. You instantly get it.

rows.Effects: Increases the amount of trees that spawn.
rows.Notes: Grants on average 0.33 trees spawned every 10 seconds.

rows.Effects: Allows you to kill trees in one hit, as if the tree had 1 HP.
rows.Notes: Trees by default have a limit, where they will die after taking 8 hits. The Shirt changes this to 1 hit instead.

rows.Effects: Spawns a Turret when you kill a tree.
rows.Notes: This is a quite powerful effect if you have engineering and some tree stats. Especially Explorer can make great use of it.
